name: Acute Decompensated Heart Failure
description: 72yo female with progressive dyspnea and peripheral edema — classic CHF exacerbation

symptoms.text: 72-year-old female presenting with progressive dyspnea on exertion over 2 weeks, now dyspneic at rest. Bilateral lower extremity edema, orthopnea (3-pillow), and paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. History of hypertension, atrial fibrillation, and prior MI 5 years ago. Currently on lisinopril, metoprolol, and furosemide. Reports medication non-compliance for past week.
symptoms.chief_complaint: Progressive dyspnea and bilateral leg swelling

ecg.interpretation: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response, LVH with repolarization changes
